hsk_beta_channel: G (fiscal externality: direct cost savings from lower drug prices in Medicaid)
notes: HSK has MC intro (Goodman-Bacon 2017) and MC adult expansions; Dranove-Ody-Starc study a within-Medicaid cost-containment reform. β enters G directly as saved drug cost per prescription. WTP channel is ambiguous (beneficiaries are taxpayers, not enrollees).

hsk_beta_channel: G (fiscal externality via reduced inappropriate prescribing → Medicare Part D savings + downstream health effects)
notes: PDMPs reduce Schedule II prescribing in Medicare Part D. WTP of patients is ambiguous (harm reduction). Fiscal channel: reduced Part D spend, offset by potential substitution.

hsk_policy_label: Health workforce / physician supply in Brazil
notes: Brazilian More Doctors program. HSK sample is US; health workforce policy not in catalog.

hsk_policy_label: Mass HI / Medigap / health insurance regulation
hsk_beta_channel: WTP via coverage take-up; G via subsidy cost
notes: Community-rating reforms in individual HI market. Adjacent to HSK's Mass HI, Medigap tax, Oregon Health but not identical (regulatory, not subsidy).

hsk_beta_channel: G (private prices, not government cost) - mostly outside MVPF framework
notes: Multimarket-contact study of non-Medicare hospital prices. Private market, MVPF framework does not naturally apply.

hsk_beta_channel: WTP (additive component: maternal self-rated health beyond earnings)
notes: HSK EITC 1993 MVPF=1.12 uses only earnings/tax channel. Evans-Garthwaite's effect on maternal health adds a distinct non-pecuniary WTP component, analogous to HSK's discussion of infant health in their §IV.B. PILOT CASE.

hsk_beta_channel: WTP (coverage expansion to young adults); G (subsidy via parent's employer plan)
notes: ACA young-adult mandate. Adjacent to Mass HI/Medicaid adult. β (coverage rate) enters WTP denominator normalization.
South African user-fee abolition. Outside HSK's US scope.
Regulation of post-partum care practices; no direct fiscal transfer or subsidy. MVPF not naturally defined.

hsk_beta_channel: WTP (future health gains); G (averted outbreak costs)
notes: Tdap requirement for middle schoolers. HSK sample has no vaccine-mandate policy; but methodology is analogous to Medicaid child expansions (Wherry et al. 2018) – upstream health investment with long-run fiscal externality.

hsk_beta_channel: WTP (future earnings if habit persists + direct child-health improvements); G (health system savings)
notes: German voucher; topic (child human capital investment) aligns with HSK Child Education, but non-US.

hsk_beta_channel: WTP (VSL × mortality reduction); G (saved health costs if US-comparable)
notes: LIC water-chlorination study. Methodology is analogous to HSK's treatment of mortality in FIU/Medicaid; channel is VSL. But non-US MVPF is not directly comparable.
State young-adult coverage laws. Same topical extension as id_65.

hsk_beta_channel: WTP (infant health as additive non-pecuniary component)
notes: HSK EITC 1986 MVPF=1.20, EITC 1993=1.12. Both use only earnings/tax channel. HMS2015 estimates EITC effect on low-birthweight, which enters WTP via Almond-Currie infant-health-to-earnings literature. PILOT CASE. HSK footnote 66 explicitly discusses 'child outcomes' as WTP addition.
Indian air/water regulations. Outside HSK's US fiscal-policy scope.
